meaning: vi.变平,变单调vt.使平,变平,打倒,使失去光泽
examples: The cookies will flatten slightly while cooking.
If you leave, for example, a bar of ice supported only at one end, the end, the unsupported end will deform under its own weight, it'll kind of flatten out at one end, get distorted, deformed.
Export growth has started to flatten out.
The purpose of the bill is simple: flatten, simplify, and reform the federal tax code.
Bringing that ratio in line with peers would flatten the cost curve for decades.
